Successfully controlled the emotions and held the trade despite the internal pressure.  This moment serves as a reference for future situations where external opinions or market chatter could trigger emotional reactions.
notes:
Timeframe & Setup:

Daily: Observed a downside daily CRT, but it lacked validation from order blocks or key level rejection. Price retraced into an upside daily breakout block, which mitigated and rejected to the upside.

4-Hour: Noted a CRT forming.

15-Minute: Marked impulsive market structure shift (MSS) and observed a break of structure approaching CRT completion.


Entry & Execution:

Entered at the body of the daily breakout block after the CRT finished retracing to that level.

Risk management: Closed halfway to maintain phase management on the Quantek account, keeping the account in the “one trade-to-pass” zone.


Notes / Takeaways:

Daily CRT opposite to the rejection confirms CRT alone isn’t enough—validation via key levels or order blocks is required.

Phase management and strategic exits help protect future trades and maintain consistency.
